## Chapter to edit (ONLY this file)

## Single task
**Expand the PROOF of the helper lemma `lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural`**
(`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_natural}`), which the prior writer
(bw253) added this iter as a statement-only stub. The progress-critic flagged that "mechanical
bookkeeping remains" is not a prover-executable specification: the chapter must carry the explicit
element-level argument so the next prover executes rather than re-derives. You are adding a
`\begin{proof}...\end{proof}` (mathematical prose, textbook level) to that lemma block.

## The mathematics to write (this is the proof of the fourth D1′ square at the helper level)

`sheafifyTensorUnitIso` is the unit/tensor reconciliation natural iso used to build the sheaf-level
comparison `pullbackTensorMap` (`lem:pullback_tensor_map`). Its naturality square in the two module
arguments is verified by descending TWICE and then checking a bilinear identity on pure tensors:

1. **Descend to sections.** By extensionality of presheaf-of-modules morphisms, the square commutes
   iff it commutes componentwise over each open `U` — an equality of `\mathcal{O}_X(U)`-linear maps
   out of `(M \otimes_X N)(U)`.

2. **Descend to elements.** By extensionality of `\ModuleCat`-morphisms, two `\mathcal{O}_X(U)`-linear
   maps out of `(M \otimes_X N)(U)` agree iff they agree on every element. Because both composites are
   `\mathcal{O}_X(U)`-linear (in particular additive), it suffices to check agreement on the
   generating **pure tensors** `m \otimes n` (`m \in M(U)`, `n \in N(U)`): the zero element and
   additivity (sums of pure tensors) cases are formal — both composites send `0 \mapsto 0` and respect
   addition — so by the universal property of the tensor product (`TensorProduct` induction on the
   element) the pure-tensor case suffices.

3. **The pure-tensor identity is the conjunction of two single-argument unit-naturalities.** On a pure
   tensor `m \otimes n`, the comparison's value factors as the tensor of the two single-argument legs.
   The square therefore reduces to the two independent naturality squares of the **sheafification unit**
   `\eta` (the unit `\mathbf{1} \Rightarrow (\text{sheafify})\circ(\text{inclusion})` of the
   sheafification adjunction), one per tensor argument:
   - first argument, evaluated at `m`:
     `\eta_{M'} \circ (\text{sheafify of the induced map}) = (\text{the induced map}) \circ \eta_M`;
   - second argument, evaluated at `n`: the analogous identity.
   Each holds simply because `\eta` is a natural transformation (its naturality square commutes by
   definition). Tensoring the two evaluated identities gives the pure-tensor equality; bilinearity
   (step 2) extends it to all of `(M \otimes_X N)(U)`, proving the square.

## Lean signposts (acceptable as "the formalization proceeds via …" pointers — NOT tactic strings)
You may name, as landmarks (no tactic blocks, no `simp only [...]`/`erw [...]` fragments):
- `PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext` (step 1 descent), `ModuleCat.hom_ext` + `TensorProduct` induction
  (step 2 descent), the sheafification-unit naturality `\eta = (\text{sheafificationAdjunction}).\text{unit}`
  (step 3), and that the identity-restriction strip `restrictScalarsId_map` reconciles the carrier
  spelling before reading off the unit naturality. Keep these to brief parenthetical pointers; the body
  is mathematical prose.

## Constraints
- This is Archon-bespoke formalization engineering — **no `% SOURCE:` / `% SOURCE QUOTE:` lines**.
- **Do NOT touch `\leanok`/`\mathlibok` markers** (sync owns `\leanok`; review owns `\mathlibok`).
- Edit ONLY the `lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural` block (add its proof). Do not touch any other
  block, including the parent `lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural` (bw253 already corrected its sketch to
  cross-reference this helper).
- Keep the proof's `\uses{}` honest: it depends on `lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso` (the iso being shown
  natural) / the sheafification unit; do not introduce a cycle by `\uses`-ing the parent D1′ lemma.

## Report
Confirm the proof body added to `lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural`, that it is math-pure (no tactic
strings), and that no markers were touched.

3290	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes. The sheaf-level comparison map
3291	  \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} = \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) of
3292	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map} is \emph{natural} in both module arguments: for
3293	  morphisms \(a : M \to M'\) and \(b : N \to N'\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
3294	  the square
3295	  \[
3296	    \begin{array}{ccc}
3297	      f^*(M \otimes_X N)
3298	        & \xrightarrow{\;\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\;}
3299	        & f^*M \otimes_Y f^*N \\[2pt]
3300	      \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f^*(a \otimes b)}
3301	        & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f^*a \,\otimes\, f^*b} \\[4pt]
3302	      f^*(M' \otimes_X N')
3303	        & \xrightarrow{\;\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\;}
3304	        & f^*M' \otimes_Y f^*N'
3305	    \end{array}
3306	  \]
3307	  commutes.
3308	\end{lemma}
3309	
3310	\begin{proof}
3311	  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal,
3312	        lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural, lem:pullback_val_iso_natural}
3313	  The map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) is built (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}) by
3314	  transporting the abstract oplax comparison \(\delta\) of the presheaf pullback
3315	  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}) through the sheafification
3316	  reconciliations. Each ingredient is natural: the abstract oplax \(\delta\) is natural
3317	  in both arguments --- this holds for any oplax monoidal functor --- and the sheafification,
3318	  the \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) comparison, and the unit/tensor reconciliation
3319	  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) are natural transformations. The asserted square is the
3320	  pasting of these naturality squares, so it commutes; naturality in the two arguments is
3321	  checked separately and then combined, the tensor of the two component morphisms factoring
3322	  as \(f^*a \otimes f^*b\) by bifunctoriality of \(\otimes_Y\).
3323	
3324	  The fourth square --- the naturality of \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\)
3325	  (\cref{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural}) --- is the one square that is \emph{not}
3326	  closed by the formal monoidal interchange (whisker-exchange) law. When the comparison is
3327	  expanded into its whisker factors, the whiskers of the sheafification unit \(\eta\) and
3328	  the factors coming from the tensor-of-morphisms \(p \otimes q\) are framed by two
3329	  monoidal structures on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) that agree on the nose but whose
3330	  syntactic mismatch prevents the interchange law from bridging between them.
3331	  The square is instead closed by \emph{descending to sections}. Applying the extensionality
3332	  principle for presheaf-of-modules morphisms (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext}\)) reduces
3333	  the square to an equality of its components over each open \(U\). Over \(U\) the whiskers
3334	  unfold to honest tensor-of-morphisms maps, and after module-level extensionality
3335	  (\(\mathtt{ModuleCat.hom\_ext}\)) the identity reduces, by \(\mathtt{TensorProduct}\)
3336	  induction, to the sectionwise naturality of \(\eta\) on each pure tensor \(p \otimes q\):
3337	  \[
3338	    p_U \circ (\eta_{M'})_U = (\eta_{M})_U \circ (f^*a)_U,
3339	    \qquad
3340	    q_U \circ (\eta_{N'})_U = (\eta_{N})_U \circ (f^*b)_U,
3341	  \]
3342	  combined bilinearly. Thus the fourth square reduces to the bilinear, sectionwise
3343	  naturality of the sheafification unit \(\eta\), verified one pure tensor at a time.
3344	\end{proof}
3345

bilinear, sectionwise naturality of the sheafification unit \(\eta\), verified one pure
  tensor \(p \otimes q\) at a time by \(\mathtt{TensorProduct}\) induction.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso}
  Write \(\Phi\) for the natural transformation whose naturality square (in the two module
  arguments \(M, N\), along morphisms \(a : M \to M'\) and \(b : N \to N'\)) is to be shown
  to commute; \(\Phi\) is assembled from the reconciliation isomorphism
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) (\cref{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso}) and so its
  components are morphisms of presheaves of modules. We close the square in three descents.

  \emph{Step 1: descend to sections.} A morphism of presheaves of modules is determined by
  its components over each open \(U\): two such morphisms agree as soon as their components
  agree over every \(U\) (the extensionality principle
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext}\)). It therefore suffices to fix an open \(U\) and to
  prove that the two composites around the square agree as \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-linear maps
  out of \((M \otimes_X N)(U)\). Before reading off the comparison componentwise one
  reconciles the carrier spelling: the identity-restriction strip
  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsId\_map}\) rewrites the restriction of scalars along the identity
  to the bare module map, so that both legs are presented as honest
  \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-linear maps with the same source and target.

  \emph{Step 2: descend to elements.} Two \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-linear maps out of the module
  \((M \otimes_X N)(U)\) agree as soon as they agree on every element (the extensionality
  principle \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.hom\_ext}\)). Each composite around the square is
  \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-linear, hence in particular additive and unit-preserving: both send
  the zero element to zero and respect addition. The module \((M \otimes_X N)(U)\) is
  generated by the pure tensors \(p \otimes q\) with \(p \in M(U)\), \(q \in N(U)\), and an
  element of a tensor product is built from pure tensors by the universal property
  (\(\mathtt{TensorProduct}\) induction): the zero and additivity cases are formal because
  both composites are additive and preserve \(0\). It therefore suffices to check that the
  two composites agree on every pure tensor \(p \otimes q\).

  \emph{Step 3: the pure-tensor identity is two unit-naturalities.} On a pure tensor
  \(p \otimes q\) the value of the comparison factors as the tensor of its two
  single-argument legs, one in the first module argument and one in the second. The
  pure-tensor equality therefore splits as the conjunction of two independent identities,
  each of which is precisely a naturality square of the sheafification unit
  \(\eta = (\mathtt{sheafificationAdjunction}).\mathrm{unit}\)
  (the unit \(\mathbf{1} \Rightarrow (\text{sheafify}) \circ (\text{inclusion})\) of the
  sheafification adjunction), evaluated at the relevant element:
  \[
    (\eta_{M'})_U \circ \big(\text{induced map}\big)_U
      = \big(\text{induced map}\big)_U \circ (\eta_{M})_U
      \quad\text{evaluated at } p,
  \]
  in the first argument, and the analogous identity in the second argument evaluated at
  \(q\). Each of these holds simply because \(\eta\) is a natural transformation, so its
  naturality square commutes by definition. Tensoring the two evaluated identities yields the
  equality of the two composites on the pure tensor \(p \otimes q\); by the bilinearity and
  pure-tensor reduction of Step 2 this extends to all of \((M \otimes_X N)(U)\), and by the
  componentwise reduction of Step 1 it proves that the naturality square of
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) commutes.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
  [Naturality of the \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) comparison]

# blueprint-writer bw253b — report

## Task
Expanded the PROOF of helper lemma `lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural`
(`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_natural}`) in
`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`. The prior writer (bw253) left it a
statement-only stub; the progress-critic flagged that "mechanical bookkeeping remains" was not
a prover-executable spec.

## What was added
A `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}` block immediately after the lemma (formerly directly followed
by `lem:pullback_val_iso_natural`). The proof gives the explicit element-level argument as the
three-descent structure specified in the directive:

1. **Descend to sections** (`PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext`) — square commutes iff componentwise
   over each open `U`; the `restrictScalarsId_map` strip reconciles the carrier spelling first.
2. **Descend to elements** (`ModuleCat.hom_ext` + `TensorProduct` induction) — both composites
   are `O_X(U)`-linear (additive, send 0→0), so the zero/additivity cases are formal and it
   suffices to check pure tensors `p ⊗ q`.
3. **Pure-tensor identity = two unit-naturalities** — the value factors as the tensor of two
   single-argument legs; each is a naturality square of the sheafification unit
   `η = (sheafificationAdjunction).unit`, evaluated at `p` (resp. `q`); tensoring + bilinearity
   extends to all of `(M ⊗_X N)(U)`.

## Compliance
- **Math-pure**: prose only. Lean names appear solely as brief parenthetical landmarks
  (`PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext`, `ModuleCat.hom_ext`, `TensorProduct`, `restrictScalarsId_map`,
  `sheafificationAdjunction.unit`). No tactic strings, no `simp only [...]`/`erw [...]` fragments.
- **No `% SOURCE:` / `% SOURCE QUOTE:` lines** (Archon-bespoke formalization).
- **No `\leanok`/`\mathlibok` markers touched** — none added, none removed.
- **Scope**: edited ONLY the `lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural` block (added its proof). The
  parent `lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural` and all other blocks untouched.
- **`\uses{}` honest & cycle-free**: the proof `\uses{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso}` (the iso
  being shown natural) plus the sheafification unit; it does NOT `\uses` the parent D1′ lemma
  `lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural`, so no cycle is introduced.
